facts_disclosed: Doesn't work weekends — family time is sacred

turns.content: had a customer ring me at 7am on saturday asking if I could come look at a leaking radiator. 7am!! on a saturday!!
7 AM on a Saturday is bold, even for a plumbing emergency. Was it actually urgent, or more of a slow drip they'd been ignoring all week and suddenly decided was critical at the weekend?

slow drip theyd been ignoring since wednesday apparently. I was polite about it but I said no. I dont do weekends mate. full stop. saturdays are for the kids, we do swimming and then usually the park or whatever. sundays are family day, me and Simran and the kids, we do something together every sunday without fail

Good for you. That's a boundary that a lot of self-employed people struggle to hold, especially when it's a paying customer on the line. But you're right to protect it -- those swimming sessions and Sunday family days are the stuff your kids will actually remember.

Did the customer take it well, or were they annoyed?
bit annoyed but I told them I'd come monday first thing and sort it. a slow drip isnt gonna flood the house over the weekend. put a towel under it and itll be fine. some people just panic innit

Exactly right. A slow drip with a towel underneath is a Monday problem, not a Saturday morning emergency. You gave them a reasonable solution and a firm Monday slot -- that's professional and fair.

Do you get weekend calls often, or is this more of a one-off?

more than youd think. people assume cos youre self employed youre available 24/7. but I made the decision years ago, weekends are off limits. Simran would kill me if I started working saturdays. and honestly even if she didnt, the money can wait. family cant. Arjun and Preet are only this age once innit

That's a really mature perspective, and honestly one that a lot of business owners never arrive at. The temptation to take every job is real, especially when you're sole trading and every call is direct income. But you're absolutely right -- Arjun and Preet won't be 8 and 5 forever.

Do you make the boundary clear upfront when you take on new customers? Like, do you mention your working hours when you give quotes?
yeah I tell them straight away. monday to friday 8 to 6, emergency callouts weekday evenings only. weekends no. most people respect it. and the ones that dont I probably dont want as customers anyway

That's the right attitude. Setting the expectation upfront avoids exactly the kind of 7 AM Saturday phone call you got. And you're right -- customers who don't respect your boundaries tend to be the ones who are difficult in other ways too.

Have you thought about putting it on your voicemail or any marketing material? Something like "Available Monday to Friday, 8-6" so it's clear before they even call?
its on my google listing and my business cards but maybe I should update the voicemail actually. good idea. something short and simple like hours are monday to friday and I'll get back to you

Perfect. Keep it simple:

"Hi, you've reached Raj at RM Plumbing & Heating. I'm available Monday to Friday, 8 AM to 6 PM. Leave a message with your name, number, and what you need, and I'll get back to you on the next working day. Cheers."

Short, clear, sets the expectation. No one can say they didn't know your hours if it's the first thing they hear. And it sounds professional too.
thats good actually. might record that later. right now I gotta go cos Preet wants to go to the park and shes giving me the look. you know the look kids give you when theyre about to kick off
Ha, I know exactly the look you mean. Go enjoy the park -- that's what Saturdays are for. Record the voicemail when you've got a quiet minute during the week. Have a good weekend with the family.
cheers. saturdays sorted sundays sorted. weekends are mine. have a good one
You too, Raj. Enjoy it -- and well done holding that boundary. Not everyone can.
